identity.name: The Move Book
identity.oneLiner: A comprehensive guide to the Move language and the Sui blockchain.

sources.quote: This is The Move Book - a comprehensive guide to the Move programming language and the Sui blockchain.
The book is intended for developers who are interested in learning about Move and building on Sui.

entities.name: Move
entities.description: An open source language for writing safe packages to manipulate onchain objects, sometimes referred to as smart contracts.

sources.quote: Move is an open source language for writing safe packages to manipulate onchain objects (sometimes referred to as _smart contracts_).

entities.name: Sui
entities.description: A blockchain whose Move implementation uses its own object-centric global storage and repurposes the address type as a 32-byte identifier.

sources.quote: Move on Sui contains some important differences from Move on other blockchains.

entities.name: The Move Book
entities.description: A comprehensive guide to the Move programming language and the Sui blockchain, intended for developers learning Move and building on Sui.

sources.quote: This is The Move Book - a comprehensive guide to the Move programming language and the Sui blockchain.

entities.name: Object
entities.description: A fundamental unit of storage on the network; every resource, asset, or piece of data onchain is an object with a globally unique ID.

sources.quote: An object is a fundamental unit of storage on the network.
Every resource, asset, or piece of data onchain is an object.

entities.name: Abilities
entities.description: A way to allow certain behaviors for a type, set in the struct definition using the has keyword followed by a list of abilities.

sources.quote: Abilities are a way to allow certain behaviors for a type.
Abilities are set in the struct definition using the has keyword followed by a list of abilities.

entities.name: key ability
entities.description: On Sui the key ability indicates that a struct is an object type and requires the first field to have signature id: UID.

sources.quote: key - allows the struct to be used as a _key_ in a storage.
indicates that a struct is an object type and comes with an additional requirement that the first field of the struct has signature

entities.name: store ability
entities.description: The store ability allows a struct to be stored in structs that have the key ability.

sources.quote: store - allows the struct to be _stored_ in structs that have the _key_ ability.

entities.name: drop ability
entities.description: The drop ability allows a struct to be dropped or discarded.

sources.quote: drop - allows the struct to be _dropped_ or _discarded_.

entities.name: Hot Potato pattern
entities.description: A struct without any abilities that cannot be stored, copied, or discarded and must be gracefully unpacked before the transaction ends.

sources.quote: A case in the abilities system - a struct without any abilities - is called _hot potato_.

entities.name: entry function
entities.description: An entry function is usable by anyone on chain but cannot be wrapped around other Move logic.

sources.quote: when you want some functionality to be usable by anyone on chain, but not be wrapped around other Move logic

entities.name: object-centric global storage
entities.description: Sui's own object-centric storage model, used instead of the global storage that is part of the programming model in Move on Diem.

sources.quote: Move on Sui addresses the scaling issue by not having global storage or its related operations.
In Move on Diem, global storage is part of the programming model.

entities.name: smart contracts
entities.description: The onchain programs Move packages implement to manipulate onchain objects, sometimes referred to as smart contracts.

sources.quote: Move is an open source language for writing safe packages to manipulate onchain objects (sometimes referred to as _smart contracts_).

claims.text: Move does not support overflow or underflow; an out-of-range operation raises a runtime error.

sources.quote: Move does not support overflow / underflow; an operation that results in a value outside the range of the type will raise a runtime error.

questions.question: What is Move?
questions.answer: Move is an open source language for writing safe packages to manipulate onchain objects, sometimes referred to as smart contracts.

sources.quote: Move is an open source language for writing safe packages to manipulate onchain objects (sometimes referred to as _smart contracts_).

questions.question: What is The Move Book?
questions.answer: It is a comprehensive guide to the Move programming language and the Sui blockchain.

sources.quote: This is The Move Book - a comprehensive guide to the Move programming language and the Sui blockchain.

questions.question: What are abilities in Move?
questions.answer: Abilities are a way to allow certain behaviors for a type, set in the struct definition using the has keyword. They include copy, drop, key, and store.

sources.quote: Abilities are a way to allow certain behaviors for a type.

questions.question: How does Sui's storage model differ from other blockchains?
questions.answer: Move on Sui addresses the scaling issue by not having global storage or its related operations, using its own object-centric storage instead of the global storage found in Move on Diem.

sources.quote: Move on Sui addresses the scaling issue by not having global storage or its related operations.

questions.question: What is the hot potato pattern?
questions.answer: A hot potato is a struct with no abilities used to ensure that some action is taken before the transaction ends, similar to a callback.

sources.quote: Hot potatoes are used to ensure that some action is taken before the transaction ends, similar to a callback.

questions.question: What does the key ability do on Sui?
questions.answer: On Sui the key ability indicates that a struct is an object type and requires the first field of the struct to have signature id: UID.

sources.quote: indicates that a struct is an object type and comes with an additional requirement that the first field of the struct has signature

questions.question: Who is The Move Book for?
questions.answer: It is intended for developers who are interested in learning about Move and building on Sui.

sources.quote: The book is intended for developers who are interested in learning about Move and building on Sui.

questions.question: What integer types does Move support?
questions.answer: Move supports unsigned integers of various sizes, from 8-bit to 256-bit (u8 through u256).

sources.quote: Move supports unsigned integers of various sizes, from 8-bit to 256-bit.

questions.question: Is The Move Book complete?
questions.answer: No. The book is in active development and a work in progress.

sources.quote: The book is in active development and a work in progress.
